content:
The rival serves as a mirror to the protagonist, showing an alternate way to pursue their craft—or the dangers of their own path.

Examples:
• Salieri (Amadeus) – The jealous rival who understands greatness but lacks the genius.
• Ryan (Whiplash) – The effortless talent, contrasting Andrew’s hard-fought skill.
• Koji Takahashi (Real) – A former athlete who resents his disability, reflecting an alternate way of coping.

Key Traits:
• Equally skilled (or naturally talented where the protagonist isn’t).
• Takes a different path—either a cautionary tale or an inspiration.
• Sometimes more well-adjusted, showing what the protagonist is missing.

4. The Love Interest (The One Who Cannot Stay)

This character often symbolizes normal life—and the protagonist almost always pushes them away.

Examples:
• Nicole (Whiplash) – Andrew dates her but chooses drumming over emotional connection.
• Paola (Raging Bull) – Married to Jake but slowly loses respect for his abusive behavior.
context: From document: Analyzing the Common Threads in Whiplash, Real, and Vagabond — chunk 12 (chunk 12/14)
